subject: hid-microsoft: support for XBox Series X/S controller.
author: Sorgelig
provenance_doc_ref: patch-provenance.md §3.3 Class C, line 338; disposition: drop

impact.effect_if_absent: Xbox Series X/S controllers (BT, PID 0x0b13) will not bind to hid-microsoft driver; force feedback (rumble) will be unavailable on these devices

notes: MiSTer commit predates upstream (2021-09-04 vs 2023-04-25) — this is a targeted prototype of the upstream solution, not a backport. Upstream commit f5554725f30475b05b5178b998366f11ecb50c7f adds the same device ID (0x0b13) to hid-ids.h with symbol USB_DEVICE_ID_MS_XBOX_CONTROLLER_MODEL_1914 (vs MiSTer's USB_DEVICE_ID_MS_XBOX_SERIES_CONTROLLER) and binds it in the SAME ms_devices[] table shape: HID_BLUETOOTH_DEVICE(USB_VENDOR_ID_MICROSOFT, <id>) with .driver_data = MS_QUIRK_FF — identical match tuple and identical quirk flag, differing only in the cosmetic #define name. For the specific device this fork commit targets (0x0b13), coverage is functionally EQUIVALENT, not partial: re-graded from "partial" to "equivalent" on sonnet-verify (grep of drivers/hid/hid-microsoft.c confirms USB_DEVICE_ID_MS_XBOX_CONTROLLER_MODEL_1914 is used nowhere else in the file — no other quirk/special-case logic keyed off this specific ID that could differ). Upstream's SAME commit additionally covers other devices (1708 firmware-update fix, 1797, 1797_BLE) that are NOT part of what this fork commit introduced — those belong to other records (1797_BLE = record adbaaea9130610effa761f958a67d82cc73422aa). CONFIG_HID_MICROSOFT=y is enabled in linux.config. No userspace-level coupling: Main_MiSTer does not reference USB_DEVICE_ID_MS_XBOX_SERIES_CONTROLLER, MODEL_1914, or the raw ID 0x045e:0x0b13 anywhere (grep of Main_MiSTer/*.cpp,*.h for "0b13"/"0B13" returns nothing). Effect if dropped: silent — feature (rumble on Series X/S BT controllers) disappears but no build/boot error occurs. sonnet-verified: confirmed disposition/upstream-existence/device-ID/userspace-coupling; corrected equivalence grade partial→equivalent (same PID, same quirk flag, no partial-coverage gap for the ID this commit added) and normalized the vanilla SHA to its full 40-character form (f5554725f30475b05b5178b998366f11ecb50c7f), confirmed an ancestor of v6.18.38 via `git merge-base --is-ancestor`.
